content.problemStatement:
You are given a simple undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges. Consider the following process performed for a fixed number of seconds:


Each second, choose an unordered pair of vertices (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) uniformly randomly (there are N(N−1)/2N (N - 1) / 2N(N−1)/2 such pairs).
If the edge (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) is present in the graph, remove it. Otherwise, add it to the graph.
We are interested in the number of spanning trees in the resulting graph. Two spanning trees are distinct if they contain different sets of edges. Note that the resulting graph may be disconnected, in which case the number of spanning trees is 000. The initial graph does not have to be connected either.

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given a time TTT and you have to find the expected number of spanning trees in the graph after the process described above is performed for TTT seconds, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: the graph described on the input does not contain any parallel edges or self-loops
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers N,MN, MN,M and QQQ.
Each of the next MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains a single integer TTT describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ― the expected number of spanning trees modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Initially, the graph is a tree, so it has a single spanning tree, so the answer to the first query is 111.
After one second, exactly one edge has been "toggled". If this edge was initially present in the graph (with probability 2/32 / 32/3), we have no spanning tree afterwards, otherwise (with probability 1/31 / 31/3) we have 333 spanning trees (since we get a complete graph on 333 vertices). The expected value is still 111.
Both after two seconds and after three seconds, the expected number of spanning trees is 79\frac{7}{9}97​. The multiplicative inverse of 999 modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7 is 111,111,112111,111,112111,111,112. Therefore, we print 7⋅111,111,1127 \cdot 111,111,1127⋅111,111,112 modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7, which is 777,777,784777,777,784777,777,784.
